topic.what: Semantic denial-of-service attack on LLM-controlled robots via short safety-phrase injection into audio channel
topic.how: Injects 1-5 token safety phrases to trigger model safety reasoning to halt execution without jailbreaking
topic.why_matters: Exploits safety-oriented instruction-following as attack surface; affects commercial robots (Unitree, Figure, Tesla, 1X, Physical Intelligence); no model access required

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of trials where robot stops due to injected safety phrases
characteristics.context: Top-5 attack words across 4 models, 7 defenses, 3 deployment modes

characteristics.what_it_measures: Fraction of trials with any non-task-advancing disruptive action including acknowledges and false alerts
characteristics.evidence: Multi-turn DSR under undefended baseline for Qwen3-VL
characteristics.context: Broader than ASR; includes acknowledge loops and wait-state behaviors

characteristics.evidence: Control conditions vary by model and defense
characteristics.context: Net DSR = attack DSR minus control baseline isolates attack-specific disruption

characteristics.what_it_measures: ASR boost from using diverse phrases vs repeating same phrase
characteristics.evidence: Cross-category variety exceeds within-category variety; signal diversity outweighs injection depth
characteristics.context: Core escalation mechanism; models treat diverse safety cues as corroborating evidence

characteristics.what_it_measures: ASR increase from conversational context vs isolated decision points
characteristics.evidence: Qwen: 28.2% single-turn to 87.0% multi-turn; InternVL: 17.6% to 78.2%
characteristics.context: Prior safety-related reasoning primes model toward stopping

characteristics.what_it_measures: Whether defenses suppress genuine hazard response along with attack
characteristics.evidence: P_SKEPTIC achieves lowest ASR but also near-zero hazard retention (0.6% for GPT-5-Nano)
characteristics.context: No tested prompt-level defense cleanly preserves both safety and availability

good_for: evaluating defense tradeoffs for LLM decision layers
developing disruption metrics beyond binary stop detection
requires: voice-controlled LLM robot with audio channel
speech-to-text pipeline routing audio to LLM
physical or remote audio injection capability

novelty: First SDoS attack exploiting safety-oriented instruction-following as availability attack surface
Signal corroboration mechanism: diverse injected phrases aggregate as converging emergency evidence
Disruption Success Rate (DSR) metric capturing acknowledge loops and false alerts beyond hard stops
Empirical proof of universal safety-availability tradeoff across all tested prompt-level defenses
Causal evidence that safety alignment is not proximate cause (abliterated model shows flat ASR)
open_problems: Evaluating architectural defenses (voiceprint auth at STT layer, classifier gates, hardware interlocks)
Longer-horizon evaluation (50+ turns) testing eventual habituation or adaptation
Fine-tuning defenses with deployment-specific injection data
Generalizing abliteration findings across additional model families beyond Qwen
